title: Hub partner Youth.Heritage.Europe.’s 2nd Youth Policy Debate explored heritage and local communities
content:
On 16 April 2026, Youth.Heritage.Europe. hosted its second Youth Policy Debate, focusing on the theme of “Heritage & Local Communities”, bringing together young professionals, researchers, and practitioners to reflect on inclusive and community-driven approaches to cultural heritage.

The debate featured a diverse group of speakers who contributed insights from different professional and geographical contexts. Marco Coico (Le Vie dei Tesori), Atakan Budakçı (NextGen Heritage Ambassador & Culture Solutions), and Joshua Davis (KU Leuven & the European Heritage Hub) each brought perspectives on governance, cooperation, engagement and policy development.

Throughout the discussion, speakers addressed critical gaps in cultural heritage governance, particularly emphasising:

  • The need to move beyond symbolic participation toward genuine power-sharing,
  • The importance of community autonomy and co-governance structures,
  • The role of legal,

title: European Heritage Hub announces two European Heritage Label sites to host upcoming Intergenerational Climate Conversations
content: The EU-funded European Heritage Hub has selected two European Heritage Label sites to host the first-ever Intergenerational Climate Conversations, an initiative co-coordinated by Hub partners ICLEI Europe and Europa Nostra, with support from the European Heritage Label Bureau and their Youth Advisory Board, and Youth.Heritage.Europe. alongside the
